rationale: The citing article is a focused commentary on Shiv, Carmon, and Ariely (2005), summarizing their experiments and repeatedly using their findings as the central empirical case. Berns uses those results to frame questions about placebo mechanisms, contrast conscious versus nonconscious expectancy effects, and develop a neuroscience interpretation. The paper's argument and proposed future fMRI tests are built around the cited study rather than merely listing it as background.
relation_description: Berns treats Shiv et al. (2005) as the core empirical foundation for his neuroscience-oriented discussion of price placebo effects. He uses their reported effects to motivate mechanistic interpretation and to propose follow-up brain-imaging tests.
